summary: This is a very brief announcement post for Episode 21 of the Stack Overflow podcast. Joel mentions that he and Jeff Atwood discussed how to handle abusive users in online communities. He uses a chess analogy to describe users who disrupt the intended purpose of a platform. The post also notes that Stack Overflow was about to launch publicly the following Monday.
key_points: Stack Overflow podcast episode 21 covered the challenge of abusive users in online communities
The core problem is framed as some users deliberately undermining the purpose of a platform while others try to use it constructively
Stack Overflow was days away from its public launch at the time of this post
money_quotes: You've got a bunch of people playing Chess, but certain people want to play 'throw the chess pieces all over the park…'
key_insight: Online communities must grapple with the fundamental tension between users who want to use a platform as intended and those who want to disrupt it.
